mnemonics: LSTM: Long Short-Term Memory. Remember it as 'Long Story, Too Many' details. Just like an LSTM can remember the context from the beginning of a long story, its gates decide which details are too many to keep.
keyPoints: LSTMs solve the vanishing gradient problem of simple RNNs.
They use a cell state acting as a 'conveyor belt' for long-term information.
Three gates (Forget, Input, Output) regulate the flow of information into, within, and out of the cell state.
commonMistakes: Confusing the cell state (long-term memory) with the hidden state (short-term memory/output).
Forgetting that all three gates are present and active at every single timestep.
Assuming the forget gate only works at the beginning; it decides what to forget at every step.
